creator: Marina Temkin
title: (No AI) With $3.5B in fresh capital, Kleiner Perkins is going all in on AI
dc:creator: Marina Temkin
content: The fundraise includes $1 billion for investing in early-stage startups, and $2.5 billion for late-stage growth businesses.
contentSnippet: The fundraise includes $1 billion for investing in early-stage startups, and $2.5 billion for late-stage growth businesses.

creator: Amanda Silberling
title: (No AI) OpenAI’s Sora was the creepiest app on your phone — now it’s shutting down
dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
content: Though the underlying Sora 2 video- and audio-generation model is scarily impressive, there was not sustained interest in an AI-only social feed.
contentSnippet: Though the underlying Sora 2 video- and audio-generation model is scarily impressive, there was not sustained interest in an AI-only social feed.

title: (No AI) OpenAI just gave up on Sora and its billion-dollar Disney deal
author: Richard Lawler
content:
![Frame from a video generated by Sora 2 showing a cowboy riding a horse on top of another horse](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/09/ai-label-1.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

A frame from a Sora 2-generated video. | Image: OpenAI

On Tuesday afternoon, OpenAI announced "We're saying goodbye to Sora," the video generation tool that it launched at the end of 2024, and centered in a massive licensing deal with Disney only a few months ago. _[The Wall Street Journal](https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/business/digital/openai-shutting-down-sora-ai-video-app-1236546187/)_ reported the move earlier, saying that OpenAI boss Sam Altman had informed staff that both the TikTok-like Sora app and API access for developers would be discontinued, with no plans to roll the feature into ChatGPT as had previously been [rumored](https://www.theinformation.com/articles/openai-plans-launch-sora-video-ai-chatgpt-strategy-shift?rc=v4bmzs).

According to _[The Hollywood Reporter](https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/business/digital/openai-shutting-down-sora-ai-video-app-1236546187/)_, as a result, the deal Disney announced in December, [saying it would invest $1 billion in OpenAI](https://www.theverge.com/news/842348/disney-openai-sora-chatgpt-images), license its characters for use …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/899850/openai-sora-ai-chatgpt)
contentSnippet:
A frame from a Sora 2-generated video. | Image: OpenAI	

On Tuesday afternoon, OpenAI announced "We're saying goodbye to Sora," the video generation tool that it launched at the end of 2024, and centered in a massive licensing deal with Disney only a few months ago. The Wall Street Journal reported the move earlier, saying that OpenAI boss Sam Altman had informed staff that both the TikTok-like Sora app and API access for developers would be discontinued, with no plans to roll the feature into ChatGPT as had previously been rumored. 
According to The Hollywood Reporter, as a result, the deal Disney announced in December, saying it would invest $1 billion in OpenAI, license its characters for use  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) Arm’s first CPU ever will plug into Meta’s AI data centers later this year
author: Richard Lawler
content:
![Stylized image showing the edge of a CPU](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/20260216_VISION25_ExplodedTight_Chip-01-16x9_16bit_v2-1200x675-1.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

After decades of only licensing its chip designs for others to use, UK-based Arm [revealed the first chip](https://newsroom.arm.com/news/arm-agi-cpu-launch) it's producing on its own, and the first customer. Dubbed the Arm AGI CPU, it's another chip designed for inference, or running the cloud processing for AI tools like AI agents that can continue to spawn more and more tasks to run at once. The first company in line to use it is Meta, which has [reportedly struggled](https://www.ft.com/content/d3b50dfc-31fa-45a8-9184-c5f0476f4504) to launch its own AI chips.

Meta [says it's both the lead partner and co-developer](https://about.fb.com/news/2026/03/meta-partners-with-arm-to-develop-new-class-of-data-center-silicon/), and plans to work on "multiple generations" of the data center CPUs, for use along with hardware from other vendors like [Nvidia](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/880513/nvidia-meta-ai-grace-vera-chips) and [AMD](https://www.theverge.com/tech/883593/amd-forges-100-billion-deal-with-meta-for-ai-chips). Arm cus …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/899823/arm-agi-cpu-meta)
contentSnippet:

Dubbed the Arm AGI CPU, it's another chip designed for inference, or running the cloud processing for AI tools like AI agents that can continue to spawn more and more tasks to run at once. The first company in line to use it is Meta, which has reportedly struggled to launch its own AI chips.
Meta says it's both the lead partner and co-developer, and plans to work on "multiple generations" of the data center CPUs, for use along with hardware from other vendors like Nvidia and AMD. Arm cus …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) Helping developers build safer AI experiences for teens

title: (No AI) Measuring progress toward AGI: A cognitive framework

title: (No AI) Pentagon’s ‘Attempt to Cripple’ Anthropic Is Troubling, Judge Says

creator: Lauren Goode
title: (No AI) Arm Is Now Making Its Own Chips
dc:creator: Lauren Goode
content: The chip design firm says Meta, OpenAI, Cerebras, and Cloudflare are among the first customers of its new artificial intelligence hardware.
